summary:
The phosphagen system quickly replenishes ATP during intense exercise through the transfer of a phosphate group from creatine phosphate to ADP. This process allows immediate restoration of ATP levels necessary for continued muscular contraction and performance. To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical aspects related directly to answering "Which...rapidly sources energy?" we get:

Phosphocreatine swiftly regenerates ATP via its ability to donate a phosphate group back into ADP under conditions where quick bursts or recovery are essential for sustained effort. Thus it meets as an ideal candidate among options presented here regarding fast-acting energetic substrates within active musculature contextually implied by 'exercising'. 

The summary has been reduced further maintaining core information pertinent specifically towards identifying correct choice based upon provided criteria about speed & efficiency needed at onset stage immediately post-depletion scenario described above.
